# Footguns

## Sync endpoint ignores ETag cache on empty payload

[Trigger]: When a client sends a POST /api/sync with an empty JSON body. [Detail]: The server logic treats an empty payload as a signal to refresh the entire project state from disk, bypassing the ETag-based cache validation that would normally skip a full sync if the disk content hasn't changed. Consequence: Unnecessary I/O overhead and potential race conditions where a client thinks it's up-to-date but triggers a full re-read of the database, potentially overwriting in-flight changes if the server is slow.

_Source: `src/routes/config.js`_

_Tags: `api`, `performance`_

## Project sync disabled by default to prevent duplicate registration

[Trigger]: When the server starts or runs periodic sync. [Detail]: The initial `syncAll` call and the interval timer were removed from `server.js` and `sync.js` exports. Projects are now only re-registered via explicit CLI commands or specific event hooks. [Consequence]: Existing projects in the database will not automatically update their metadata or knowledge entries on server restart, leading to stale project states until manually triggered.

_Source: `src/knowledge/extract.js`_

_Tags: `backend`, `config`_
